content:
$AUTOMATION_ENGINE is an abstract scheduler and event router. It watches for events such as:

New session start and end.
Declaration of a new world or project.
Creation or update of naming conventions.
Merge of a feature branch into dev or main.
Periodic continuity maintenance waves.

For each event, $AUTOMATION_ENGINE invokes predefined workflows described in $PROMPT_ROOT/workflow prompts and implemented as scripts in os-foundation. The exact mechanism (cron, event bus, agent framework) is left intentionally unspecified, but all workflows must be documented in WORKFLOW-layer specs and stored within $OS_ROOT/specs and $REPO_ROOT/$ORG_ID/os-foundation.

────────────────────────
3. Procedural Generation and Self-Evolving Flows

3.1 Core Procedural Generation Stages

The procedural generation layer supports three world types: conceptual, narrative, and operational. All share a common sequence of AI-callable stages:

Seed World
Expand Entities
